name: Lorca and Nogalte Castles Loop
name_original: Castillos de Lorca y Nogalte — vuelta por la Región de Murcia
description: Cycle 26.0 miles through Murcia to explore the historic Castillos de Lorca and Nogalte, a moderate ride with 994 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Lorca offer a diverse landscape, ranging from coastal views to mountainous terrain. The region features a mix of well-paved surfaces and some unpaved segments, catering to various preferences. Cyclists can explore areas with moderate elevation gains, agricultural plains, and routes that incorporate historical points of interest. This varied topography provides opportunities for both relaxed rides and more challenging ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Lorca The most popular touring cycling route is Lorca and Nogalte Castles Loop, a 26.0 miles (41.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route allows cyclists to explore historic castles. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Parazuelos Beach – Percheles Beach loop from Puntas de Calnegre, an easy 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This route offers scenic coastal views of Parazuelos and Percheles beaches. Local touring cyclists also love the Percheles Beach – Women's Bath Beach loop from Playa del Siscal, a 12.1 miles (19.5 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes and beaches, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Touring cycling around Lorca is defined by varied terrain, including coastal roads, agricultural plains, and routes with moderate elevation gains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal loops to more challenging rides. The routes in Lorca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lorca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Lorca?
A: Lorca offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 120 options available. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy coastal loops to more challenging ascents through mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Lorca?
A: The touring cycling routes around Lorca feature a diverse landscape. You'll find a mix of well-paved surfaces and some unpaved segments. The terrain ranges from scenic coastal roads with minimal traffic to agricultural plains and areas with moderate elevation gains, including ascents into the Sierra de Almenara.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Lorca?
A: Yes, Lorca has several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33753404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Parazuelos Beach – Percheles Beach loop from Puntas de Calnegre&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 8.0-mile (12.9 km) path offering scenic coastal views. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27629010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baño de las Mujeres Beach – Women's Bath Beach loop from Puntas de Calnegre&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.5 miles (12.1 km) long.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Lorca?
A: Experienced cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes in Lorca, with over 70 difficult tours available. The region's varied topography includes routes with significant elevation gains, such as ascents to Campo López via the Sierra de Almenara, providing a good test for fitness and skill.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Lorca?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Lorca are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016781308/lorca-and-nogalte-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lorca and Nogalte Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 26.0-mile (41.9 km) route that lets you explore historic castles while enjoying a full circuit.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Lorca's touring cycling routes often incorporate historical and cultural points of interest. You can explore historic castles, such as those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016781308/lorca-and-nogalte-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lorca and Nogalte Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable landmarks in the area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/535410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cope Tower&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/705186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Eulalia&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4063466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España and Collegiate Church of San Patricio&lt;/a&gt; in Lorca itself.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I discover while cycling in Lorca?
A: The region around Lorca is rich in natural beauty and offers several scenic viewpoints. Cyclists can enjoy coastal vistas, dramatic rock formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/998557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolnuevo Sandstone Formations&lt;/a&gt;, and explore areas like the Sierra Espuña Regional Park. Highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3141198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cocón de Cope&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loma de las Lagunas&lt;/a&gt; offer panoramic views of the landscape.

Q: Are there any greenways or dedicated cycling paths in the area?
A: Yes, you can find dedicated cycling paths in the Lorca region. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38171723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Almendricos – Almendricos Greenway loop&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a pleasant route away from traffic. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4118046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;EuroVelo 8 Coastal Trail at Puntas de Calnegre&lt;/a&gt; offers a segment of a major European cycling network.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Lorca?
A: The touring cycling routes in Lorca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 170 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, which includes beautiful coastal views and opportunities to explore both natural landscapes and historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal loops to more challenging rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Lorca?
A: Lorca is recognized as a preferred destination for cycling holidays, suggesting favorable conditions for much of the year. The region's climate generally allows for comfortable cycling outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn typically offer mild temperatures, making them ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Lorca?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not explicitly detailed, the region's diverse offerings include easier coastal loops and segments of the Sierra Espuña Regional Park that are suitable for families. These routes often feature well-paved surfaces and less challenging terrain, making them enjoyable for all ages.

Q: Can I find routes that combine coastal views with other landscapes?
A: Absolutely. Lorca's touring cycling routes are known for their diverse landscapes, often combining stunning coastal views with other terrains. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40367091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Percheles Beach – Women's Bath Beach loop from Playa del Siscal&lt;/a&gt; lead through coastal landscapes and beaches, while others transition into agricultural plains or areas with moderate elevation gains, offering a varied cycling experience.
tips: At the foot of the Castle of Lorca we find this beautiful viewpoint to the beautiful city of Lorca and also behind us, to the Castle. Without a doubt, it is worth stopping to contemplate the views and take some pictures.

Located in the southwest of the Region of Murcia, Lorca offers a great diversity of attractions such as the historic center, the Castle, the Synagogue or the Old Collegiate Church of San Patricio. Without a doubt, it is worth getting lost in this city. The views of Lorca from the Fortaleza del Sol are beautiful.

From the other side of the Rambla de Nogalte, we are surprised by a fortification on top of a hill. It is the Castillo de Nogalte, a fortress from the Islamic medieval era, located in the old town of Puerto Lumbreras and built during the 13th century.

faqs.question: What is the estimated cycling time for this route?
faqs.answer: The estimated cycling time for the Castillos de Lorca y Nogalte return trip is approximately 2 hours and 37 minutes for the 41.9 km distance. This duration is for active cycling and does not include stops for sightseeing at the castles or other points of interest.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a 'moderate bike ride' requiring 'good fitness'. While primarily on paved surfaces, it includes significant elevation changes, such as the climb to Lorca Castle with sections reaching an 11.4% gradient. It's not typically recommended for absolute beginners but is manageable for cyclists with some experience and a reasonable fitness level.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Castillos de Lorca y Nogalte cycling route?
faqs.answer: Specific trailhead parking information is not detailed, but as a return trip, you would typically start and end in a town like Lorca or Puerto Lumbreras, where you can find public parking. For visiting Lorca Castle, there are parking options available in Lorca town, from which you can begin your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring bicycles. However, be prepared for rolling hills and some steeper ascents, particularly when approaching the castles. The climb to Lorca Castle, for instance, has an average gradient of 5.2% over 1.2 km, with some sections reaching 11.4%.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Region of Murcia is known for its excellent climate, allowing for cycling almost all year round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while summer can be hot but still enjoyable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons. Winter is generally mild and suitable for cycling.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks and sights will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in history. You will encounter the impressive Lorca Castle, a fortress with over 250 years of border surveillance history. You'll also pass by the Collegiate Church of San Patricio of Lorca and the 13th-century Islamic medieval fortress of Nogalte Castle in Puerto Lumbreras. Beyond the castles, expect varied landscapes typical of the Murcia region, including arid plains and agricultural areas.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino del Alto Guadalentín, Transmurciana 09, and smaller local roads like Calle Canal de Riego and Atrio de San Pedro.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is on public roads and paths, the suitability for dogs depends on your specific setup (e.g., dog trailer). Always ensure your dog is safe and comfortable, especially on paved surfaces and during climbs. Check specific rules for dogs if you plan to enter any of the castle grounds or historical sites.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits are required to cycle the route itself, as it uses public roads and paths. However, there may be entrance fees if you wish to enter and explore the interiors of Lorca Castle or Nogalte Castle.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or resupply along the route?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through or near towns like Lorca and Puerto Lumbreras, you will find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and shops to resupply with food and water. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water, especially during warmer months, as stretches between towns might be less serviced.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring bicycle route, essential items include a well-maintained touring bicycle, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire (layers are good for varying temperatures), sufficient water, snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tools), and sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses, hat). Given the historical sites, a camera is also recommended.

faqs.question: Is there a particularly challenging section on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the climb to Lorca Castle is considered one of the more challenging segments. It's a 1.2 km ascent with an average gradient of 5.2% and some sections reaching 11.4%. While the overall route is moderate, this specific climb requires a good effort and contributes significantly to the route's difficulty rating.

waypoints.name: Collegiate Church of San Patricio of Lorca
